title: UN resolution urges reparations for slavery’s ‘historical wrongs’
content: Applause erupted in the UN General Assembly Hall on Wednesday as Member States adopted a resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: The UN housing development which challenged 1940s’ segregated United States
content: At a time when some state laws dictated where different races could live, Parkway Village, built to house some of the first UN staff in New York in 1947, led the way in eliminating racially segregated housing in the United States.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: ‘No matter what’, UN Women vows to stay in Afghanistan, amid deepening rights crisis
content: UN Women will continue delivering for Afghan women and girls despite sweeping restrictions and ongoing instability, a senior official said on Tuesday.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Guterres urges action against ‘rising tide of anti-Muslim hatred’
content: The UN chief on Monday urged countries to “work together” and eradicate a rising tide of anti-Muslim hate, calling for a rejection of “the narratives of fear and exclusion”.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert
content: More than five years after Myanmar’s military coup, international resolve to hold the junta accountable must not weaken, an independent human rights expert warned on Friday, as escalating violence and growing humanitarian needs push millions of civilians deeper into crisis.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Venezuela’s repressive State apparatus ‘intact’ despite leadership change
content: Venezuela’s repressive State apparatus remains operational despite the seizure of former President Nicolás Maduro by United States forces on 3 January, according to independent UN human rights investigators.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Nicaragua: Rights experts uncover State corruption fuelling repression, spying on exiles
content: Nicaragua’s Government is financing the repression of its opponents through illegal misuse of public funds and targeting exiles through a transnational surveillance and intelligence network, the UN Group of Independent Human Rights Experts on Nicaragua warned in its latest report on Tuesday.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: World News in Brief: Myanmar airstrikes worsen crisis, South Sudan fighting threatens Akobo, Afghan women shut out of justice
content: Conflict continues to fuel suffering and drive humanitarian needs in Myanmar, the UN relief coordination office, OCHA, said in a bulletin on Monday.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Iran crisis: Schoolgirls killed, thousands displaced and aid compromised
content: On the fourth day of Israeli and United States airstrikes against Iran and amid growing violence and instability in the Middle East, the UN urgently called for protection of civilians and warned of growing displacement and humanitarian needs.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Forgotten conflict in South Sudan at ‘a dangerous point’, Türk warns
content: South Sudan was the focus of debate in the UN Human Rights Council on Friday as escalating violence and political tensions – alongside a massive humanitarian emergency and war in neighbouring Sudan – threaten efforts to achieve lasting peace.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Fears grow for ordinary Afghans after further clashes with Pakistan
content: Reports on Friday that major cities in Afghanistan have been bombed by the Pakistan military in a new escalation between the two countries have raised fears for civilians already struggling under the harsh rule of the de facto Taliban authorities.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Human Rights Council hears of ongoing risk of further genocidal violence in Sudan
content: Nearly three years of war in Sudan have been marked by killings, rape and other violations, with risk of genocidal violence spreading, the UN Human Rights Council heard on Thursday.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Palestine: UN rights chief highlights suffering, atrocity crimes ‘that remain unpunished’
content: The UN rights chief Volker Türk on Thursday highlighted the “human-made disaster” across the Occupied Palestinian Territory stemming from Israel’s disregard for human rights norms and serious violations also committed by Hamas and other Palestinian armed groups.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: UN’s Türk urges dialogue after deadly clashes on Afghan-Pakistan border
content: UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Thursday appealed for dialogue between Afghanistan and Pakistan amid border clashes and deadly airstrikes, while condemning ever harsher “apartheid” edicts issued by the Afghan de facto authorities that continue to severely impact women and girls.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: World News in Brief: Arab economies rise, rights experts call for police reform in India, Ukraine school closures, Myanmar airstrikes
content: A new UN report forecasts that the Arab region is seeing a gradual economic recovery despite continuing geopolitical uncertainties.
